headline: Dawkins Issues Kill-or-Be-Killed Warning as Bills Expand Radio Reach
writeup: The Buffalo Bills are entering 2026 with a championship-or-bust mentality, as offensive lineman Dion Dawkins sounded the alarm that Josh Allen remains the cornerstone but every teammate must step up around him — a message that looms large over a training camp brimming with position battles, including a potentially explosive WR3 competition that was further complicated by the waiver claim of former Jets receiver Quentin Skinner, whose injury history is already drawing scrutiny. New head coach Joe Brady is expected to unleash a pass-heavy offense that could unlock Josh Allen's full potential, with DJ Moore's arrival potentially reshaping the Bills' formations and Dawson Knox looking to reassert himself at tight end, while linebacker Kaleb Elarms-Orr and several under-the-radar rookies hope to emerge as surprise contributors. Off the field, the Bills made waves by dramatically expanding their radio footprint, announcing 23 network affiliates for the 2026 season and strategically planting their signal in the New York markets of the Jets, Giants, and even the Dolphins, a move that has been called one of the boldest in NFL radio history. Meanwhile, Allen continues to command the league's top QB ranking, trade rumors swirling around a $30 million Bills receiver are drawing attention from Dallas and Kansas City, questions about Keon Coleman's long-term future in Buffalo persist, and the team is also celebrating community milestones including the launch of the Buffalo Legacy Trail and Alumni Park.
